title: Update working memory from extension rename session
summary: Durable context from the session that renamed Chrome-visible extension copy to Voyager while deliberately leaving persistence, compatibility identifiers, GitHub docs, and sponsor artifacts on the legacy Gemini Voyager wording.

claims.text: The packaged extension name is localized: `manifest.json` sets `name` to `__MSG_extName__`, and the default English locale resolves `extName` and `pm_title` to `Voyager`.
The in-page Prompt Manager title and representative export footers use `Voyager` as the visible product name while still linking to the `gemini-voyager` GitHub repository.
Google Drive sync still uses the legacy Drive folder name `Gemini Voyager Data` and `gemini-voyager-*.json` filenames; the current service does not rename the backup folder to `Voyager Data`.

Compatibility identifiers were not renamed with the user-facing product copy: the Firefox add-on ID remains `gemini-voyager@nagi-ovo`, and Safari converter guidance still uses bundle identifier `com.nagi-ovo.Gemini-Voyager`.

For the trademark-complaint rename, keep the scope to the minimal store-visible and in-extension user-facing surfaces needed to present the extension as `Voyager`; do not treat this as a full GitHub/docs/repository rebrand.

The session established that the Chrome Web Store display name should be changed through the manifest localization chain `manifest.name` -> `__MSG_extName__` -> locale `extName`; `manifest.json` itself does not need a literal `Voyager` name string.
The session rejected renaming the Google Drive backup folder to `Voyager Data` or adding a migration from `Gemini Voyager Data`; Drive persistence names should stay stable for this rename.
The session explicitly excluded generated sponsor assets and sponsor-generation scripts from the rename, because the user wanted only the most necessary plugin-facing changes.

The store-facing rename should not change stable extension identifiers such as the Chrome key-derived extension ID, Firefox add-on ID, or Safari bundle identifier, because those identifiers affect update continuity or signing rather than visible product naming.

sources.title: Extension rename and UI copy session

metadata.reason: The user asked to rename plugin-visible `Gemini Voyager` appearances to `Voyager` for a trademark complaint, explicitly said GitHub did not need changing, and later narrowed the work to only the most necessary changes.

The user asked whether the manifest needed changing and later confirmed the manifest question; the accepted answer was that `manifest.json` already points at `__MSG_extName__`, so changing the locale message updates the store-visible name.

After an initial broader rename included `GoogleDriveSyncService`, the user asked whether Google Drive needed changing; the accepted follow-up restored `BACKUP_FOLDER_NAME` to `Gemini Voyager Data` and removed the migration.
The user explicitly said generated sponsor-related files did not need changing and that this session should do only the most necessary work.
The user asked whether the item ID would remain stable after renaming; the session preserved extension identifiers rather than treating them as visible branding copy.
